caseMetadata.title: The Clock in the Locked Study
caseMetadata.shortDescription: A valuable pocket watch vanishes from a locked study while only three people are present.
caseMetadata.notes: First Quick Mystery engine test package. Not yet validated.

playerConfig.settingSummary: A single locked study in a mid-century private townhouse

setting.place: The locked study of Rowan Vale's townhouse
setting.dateOrSeason: An autumn evening shortly after eight o'clock
setting.socialContext: A well-kept private townhouse where household staff and family still follow formal habits.
setting.technologyLevel: Mid-century domestic technology: electric lamps, mechanical clocks, paper records, no modern forensic tools.
setting.toneNotes: Light classic-detective tone with tidy clues and a contained social puzzle.
setting.sensoryStyle: Polished wood, green-shaded lamp light, clock oil, old paper, rain tapping at the window.
setting.languageStyle: Clear, lightly period-flavored prose without heavy dialect.

centralMystery.openingProblem: Rowan Vale's rare Breguet pocket watch has vanished from a locked glass display case during a small gathering in his study.
centralMystery.stakes: The watch is a family heirloom, insured for a large sum, and scheduled to be donated to a museum the next morning.
centralMystery.playerObjective: Determine who took the watch, how it was taken, and where it is hidden.
centralMystery.successCondition: The player identifies Thomas Greer as the thief, explains the loosened hinge trick, and finds the watch hidden inside the tallcase clock's pendulum compartment.

solution.motive: Thomas Greer needed quick money to settle a gambling debt before his creditor exposed him and cost him his position.
solution.motiveProportionality: The crime is theft, not murder. The watch is valuable enough to solve Thomas's immediate debt crisis, and he believed suspicion would fall on Celia because of her pawnshop envelope.
solution.method: Earlier that day, Thomas loosened the rear hinge screws of the display case while polishing the study. During the evening gathering, he stood near the case, lifted the loosened back edge just enough to remove the watch, and hid it inside the tallcase clock's pendulum compartment.
solution.opportunity: Thomas was present in the locked study during the gathering and was expected to move around the room serving tea and adjusting the fire, giving him a plausible reason to pass near both the display case and the clock.
solution.means: A small valet's screwdriver, access to clock oil, familiarity with the tallcase clock, and the cover of normal household duties.
solution.concealmentPlan: Thomas planned to retrieve the watch after the household quieted, then let Celia take blame because her pawnshop envelope made her appear financially desperate.
solution.culpritMistake: He left black livery thread on the display hinge, transferred clock oil to the case screw, and jammed the watch in the pendulum compartment, stopping the clock at 8:13.

proofRequiredForAccusation.deductionRequired: The player must connect the tampered display hinge to Thomas's tools and duties, then connect the stopped clock to the hiding place.
proofRequiredForAccusation.insufficientAccusationResponse: The theory points toward Thomas, but the accusation is not firm until the watch is found inside the clock or the hinge evidence is connected to his livery and clock oil.

finalRevealBeats: The front lock was not defeated; the rear hinge was loosened earlier.
The black thread came from Thomas Greer's livery cuff.
The clock oil on the hinge screw points to the person who tends the tallcase clock.
The clock stopped at 8:13 because the hidden watch interfered with the pendulum compartment.
The pawnshop envelope made Celia look suspicious, but it concerns her own bracelet, not the stolen watch.
Thomas stole the watch to cover a gambling debt and intended to retrieve it later.

postResolutionAnswers.Why did Thomas take it?: He needed money quickly to pay a gambling debt and preserve his position.
postResolutionAnswers.How did he take it from the locked case?: He loosened the rear hinge beforehand and removed the watch from the back edge, leaving the front lock apparently untouched.
postResolutionAnswers.Where was the watch hidden?: Inside the pendulum compartment of the tallcase clock.
postResolutionAnswers.Why was Celia suspicious?: Her pawnshop envelope suggested financial trouble, but it concerned her own bracelet and was unrelated to the watch.

characters.name: Rowan Vale
characters.role: Owner of the watch and client
characters.relationshipToMystery: The watch belongs to him and vanished from his display case during his private gathering.
characters.apparentMotive: He could benefit from insurance if the watch disappeared before donation.
characters.trueMotiveOrInnocentExplanation: Rowan is vain and anxious, but he genuinely intended to donate the watch to a museum. He did not steal it.
characters.alibiClaim: He remained beside his writing desk after showing the watch to Celia and Thomas.
characters.alibiTruth: He stayed mostly near the desk and did not approach the clock or the display case after the watch was shown.
knowledge: The watch was in the case at 7:55 p.m.
The study door was closed during the gathering.
Thomas winds the tallcase clock every evening.
Celia had recently asked about selling family jewelry.
secrets: He exaggerated the watch's museum importance to impress Celia.
liesOrOmissions: He initially omits that Celia asked about selling jewelry because he finds it embarrassing.
characters.interrogationGuidance: Rowan is flustered, pompous, and cooperative. He can confirm Thomas's clock duties and Celia's financial worry, but he should not solve the case for the player.

characters.name: Celia Vale
characters.relationshipToMystery: She attended the gathering and had an apparent money problem.
characters.apparentMotive: A pawnshop envelope suggests she needed money and may have wanted the valuable watch.
characters.trueMotiveOrInnocentExplanation: Celia pawned her own bracelet to pay art-school fees. She did not steal the watch and is embarrassed by the envelope.
characters.alibiClaim: She sat near the fireplace and did not touch the display case.
characters.alibiTruth: She handled a teacup near the fireplace and briefly examined the case earlier when Rowan showed the watch, but she did not tamper with it.
knowledge: Thomas passed between the tea tray and the tallcase clock shortly before the theft was discovered.
The clock sounded odd after Thomas moved near it.
The pawnshop envelope belongs to her but is not connected to the watch.
secrets: She pawned her own bracelet to pay art-school fees.
liesOrOmissions: She first claims the pawnshop envelope is merely a receipt for stationery.
characters.interrogationGuidance: Celia is defensive about money but not malicious. Once gently pressed, she admits the pawnshop envelope concerns her bracelet.

characters.name: Thomas Greer
characters.relationshipToMystery: He serves in the study, tends the clock, and is the true thief.
characters.apparentMotive: He appears dutiful and has no obvious motive at first.
characters.trueMotiveOrInnocentExplanation: He owes a gambling debt and planned to sell the watch quietly after suspicion settled on Celia.
characters.alibiClaim: He says he only served tea and never touched the display case or the clock after winding it before the gathering.
characters.alibiTruth: He loosened the case hinge earlier, removed the watch during the gathering, hid it in the tallcase clock, and lied about going near the clock afterward.
knowledge: The display case rear hinge was loose because he loosened it.
The watch is hidden in the tallcase clock.
Celia's pawnshop envelope made her a useful scapegoat.
secrets: He has a gambling debt due within two days.
He owns the small screwdriver that fits the display hinge screws.
liesOrOmissions: He denies touching the clock after winding it.
He denies knowing about Celia's pawnshop envelope, though he saw it earlier.
characters.interrogationGuidance: Thomas is polite, precise, and too controlled. He should answer narrowly. If confronted with the thread, oil, and hidden watch, he falters.

locations.name: Rowan Vale's locked study
locations.description: A warm, polished study with a writing desk, fireplace, locked display case, tallcase clock, tea trolley, green-shaded lamp, and rain-streaked window.
locations.purpose: The entire mystery occurs in this room. All evidence, interviews, and final proof are available here.

timeline.summary: Thomas receives pressure from a creditor and decides he needs quick money.
timeline.cause: Thomas's gambling debt is due within two days.
timeline.effect: He identifies Rowan's valuable pocket watch as a tempting target.
timeline.visibilityToPlayer: Hidden background motive; discoverable only through Thomas's behavior or confession after proof.

timeline.summary: Thomas loosens the rear hinge screws of the display case while polishing the study.
timeline.cause: He needs a way to remove the watch without opening the front lock.
timeline.effect: The front lock remains intact, but the rear edge can be lifted just enough to remove the watch.
timeline.visibilityToPlayer: Discoverable by inspecting the display case carefully.

timeline.summary: Rowan shows the watch in the display case to Celia while Thomas serves tea.
timeline.cause: Rowan is proud of the watch and its planned museum donation.
timeline.effect: All three characters know the watch is present shortly before the theft.
witnessKnowledgeCreated: Rowan confirms the watch was present at 7:55 p.m.
Celia confirms Thomas moved freely around the room serving tea.

timeline.summary: Thomas removes the watch through the loosened rear hinge while crossing near the display case.
timeline.cause: He uses a normal serving movement as cover.
timeline.effect: The watch vanishes while the front lock remains untouched.
witnessKnowledgeCreated: Celia notices Thomas move near the clock after passing the display case.
timeline.visibilityToPlayer: Inferred from interviews and physical clues.

timeline.summary: Thomas hides the watch inside the tallcase clock's pendulum compartment, causing the clock to stop.
timeline.cause: He needs a temporary hiding place inside the locked room.
timeline.effect: The clock stops at 8:13 and the latch is freshly scraped.
witnessKnowledgeCreated: Thomas knows the clock stopped before anyone else draws attention to it.
timeline.visibilityToPlayer: Discoverable by inspecting or listening to the clock.

timeline.summary: Rowan notices the watch is missing and summons the player.
timeline.cause: Rowan returns to the case to admire the watch before locking it away for the night.
timeline.effect: The investigation begins with all three NPCs still in the study.
witnessKnowledgeCreated: The room was closed and all suspects were still present.

clues.title: Loose rear hinge
clues.playerFacingDescription: The display case's front lock is intact, but the rear hinge has a faint wobble.
clues.trueMeaning: The watch was removed through the loosened rear edge, not through the front lock.
clues.discoveryConditions: Inspect the display case carefully, especially the rear edge or hinge.
clues.sourceOrProvenance: Thomas loosened the hinge during afternoon polishing.
clues.resolvedInFinalReveal: The front lock was a distraction; Thomas prepared the rear hinge earlier.
clues.textFallback: The front lock is untouched, but the rear hinge is loose enough to admit a careful hand.

clues.title: Fine brass dust
clues.playerFacingDescription: A small crescent of brass dust rests on the green blotter below the display case hinge.
clues.trueMeaning: The hinge screws were recently disturbed.
clues.discoveryConditions: Inspect the blotter or surface beneath the display case.
clues.sourceOrProvenance: Created when Thomas loosened the hinge screws.
clues.resolvedInFinalReveal: The brass dust proves recent tampering rather than an old loose hinge.
clues.textFallback: The dust is bright and fresh, not darkened like old cabinet grit.

clues.title: Black wool thread
clues.playerFacingDescription: A single black wool thread is caught on the display case hinge.
clues.trueMeaning: It came from Thomas's black livery cuff when he worked the loosened hinge.
clues.discoveryConditions: Inspect the hinge or ask to compare the thread to clothing in the room.
clues.sourceOrProvenance: Snagged from Thomas's livery while he manipulated the hinge.
clues.resolvedInFinalReveal: The thread links the tampered hinge to Thomas rather than Celia or Rowan.
clues.textFallback: The thread is the same black wool as Thomas's livery cuff, not Celia's blue dress or Rowan's brown jacket.

clues.title: Smell of clock oil
clues.playerFacingDescription: The hinge screw carries a faint smell of clock oil.
clues.trueMeaning: The person who handled the hinge also handled the clock oil used by Thomas.
clues.discoveryConditions: Smell or closely inspect the hinge screw, or compare it with the oil cloth by the tallcase clock.
clues.sourceOrProvenance: Transferred from Thomas's fingers or cloth after he tended the clock.
clues.resolvedInFinalReveal: The clock oil connects the hinge tampering to Thomas's clock duties.
clues.textFallback: The same sharp machine-oil smell is present on the clock cloth Thomas uses.

clues.title: Clock stopped at 8:13
clues.playerFacingDescription: The tallcase clock has stopped at 8:13, although Rowan says Thomas wound it before the gathering.
clues.trueMeaning: The hidden watch jammed the pendulum compartment and stopped the clock.
clues.discoveryConditions: Look at or ask about the tallcase clock.
clues.sourceOrProvenance: The clock stopped when Thomas hid the watch inside the pendulum compartment.
clues.resolvedInFinalReveal: The stopped clock reveals the hiding place and time of concealment.
clues.textFallback: The clock hands rest at 8:13, several minutes before Rowan noticed the theft.

clues.title: Fresh scrape on the clock latch
clues.playerFacingDescription: The small latch on the clock's pendulum door has a fresh bright scrape.
clues.trueMeaning: The pendulum compartment was opened recently and hurriedly.
clues.discoveryConditions: Inspect the tallcase clock's pendulum door or latch.
clues.sourceOrProvenance: Created when Thomas opened the clock to hide the watch.
clues.resolvedInFinalReveal: The scrape shows the clock was opened during the theft window, not merely wound earlier.
clues.textFallback: The scrape is bright against older, dull scratches.

clues.title: Ticking in the pendulum compartment
clues.playerFacingDescription: A faint secondary ticking can be heard inside the stopped clock's lower compartment.
clues.trueMeaning: The missing pocket watch is hidden inside the clock.
clues.discoveryConditions: Listen closely to the stopped clock, open the pendulum compartment, or search the clock after noticing related clues.
clues.sourceOrProvenance: Thomas hid the still-running watch inside the pendulum compartment.
clues.resolvedInFinalReveal: Finding the watch in Thomas's normal work area completes the proof.
clues.textFallback: Inside the pendulum compartment, wrapped in a polishing cloth, is the missing watch.

clues.title: Thomas mentions the stopped clock too early
clues.playerFacingDescription: Thomas says, 'I had wound the clock properly; it should not have stopped,' before anyone asks him about the clock.
clues.trueMeaning: Thomas is too aware of the clock because he used it as the hiding place.
clues.discoveryConditions: Question Thomas about his movements or about the clock.
clues.sourceOrProvenance: Thomas is trying to preempt suspicion but reveals knowledge of the clock problem.
clues.resolvedInFinalReveal: His premature mention of the stopped clock shows guilty attention to the hiding place.
clues.textFallback: Thomas volunteers a defense about the clock before the player has accused him of anything.

clues.title: Celia's pawnshop envelope
clues.playerFacingDescription: A small pawnshop envelope peeks from Celia's handbag.
clues.trueMeaning: Celia pawned her own bracelet to pay art-school fees; it has nothing to do with the watch.
clues.discoveryConditions: Notice Celia's handbag, question her finances, or ask about her nervousness.
clues.sourceOrProvenance: Celia brought the envelope with her and tried to hide it out of embarrassment.
clues.resolvedInFinalReveal: The pawnshop envelope explains Celia's nervousness but not the theft.
clues.textFallback: The pawn ticket describes a pearl bracelet, not a pocket watch.

redHerrings.title: Celia's pawnshop envelope
redHerrings.appearsToSuggest: Celia is short of money and may have stolen the watch to sell it.
redHerrings.innocentExplanation: She pawned her own bracelet for art-school fees and is ashamed to tell Rowan.
redHerrings.discoveryConditions: Notice or ask about the envelope in Celia's handbag.
redHerrings.clearanceConditions: Read the ticket or press Celia gently until she admits it concerns a bracelet.
redHerrings.fairnessNotes: The envelope creates suspicion but can be cleared directly and does not outweigh the physical evidence against Thomas.

evidence.title: Locked glass display case
evidence.description: A small glass case with an intact front lock and a loosened rear hinge.
chainOfCustody: Owned by Rowan Vale
Polished by Thomas Greer earlier that day
Observed by all three NPCs during the gathering
accessHistory: Rowan holds the normal front key
Thomas had physical access while polishing
Thomas loosened the rear hinge before the gathering
evidence.discoveryConditions: Visible from the opening scene; details require inspection.

evidence.title: Display case hinge screw
evidence.description: A small hinge screw, freshly disturbed and faintly oily.
chainOfCustody: Part of the display case
Loosened by Thomas Greer
Still in place when the player investigates
accessHistory: Accessible to anyone polishing the case

evidence.title: Black wool thread
evidence.description: A single black wool fiber caught on the display case hinge.
chainOfCustody: Came from Thomas Greer's livery cuff
Caught on the display hinge
Remains there until discovered
accessHistory: Only transferable by someone contacting the rear hinge
evidence.discoveryConditions: Inspect the hinge or compare clothing fibers.

evidence.title: Clock oil cloth
evidence.description: A small cloth used for oiling the tallcase clock mechanism.
chainOfCustody: Kept near the tallcase clock
Used by Thomas Greer as part of his duties
accessHistory: Could transfer scent to his fingers or tools
evidence.discoveryConditions: Inspect the clock area or ask who tends the clock.

evidence.title: Tallcase clock
evidence.description: A tallcase clock stopped at 8:13 with a scratched pendulum-compartment latch.
chainOfCustody: Owned by Rowan Vale
Wound and maintained by Thomas Greer
Used by Thomas as a hiding place
accessHistory: Thomas has normal access for clock duties
Opened by Thomas during the theft window
evidence.discoveryConditions: Visible from the opening scene; clues require inspection or listening.

evidence.title: Breguet pocket watch
evidence.description: A rare gold pocket watch hidden inside the tallcase clock's pendulum compartment.
chainOfCustody: Owned by Rowan Vale
Displayed in glass case at 7:55 p.m.
Removed by Thomas Greer through loosened rear hinge
Hidden in the tallcase clock at 8:13 p.m.
accessHistory: Recoverable by opening the clock compartment
evidence.discoveryConditions: Listen to or open the stopped clock's pendulum compartment.

evidence.title: Celia's pawnshop envelope
evidence.description: A pawnshop envelope concerning Celia's pearl bracelet, not the stolen watch.
chainOfCustody: Received by Celia after pawning her bracelet
Carried in Celia's handbag
Visible during the investigation
accessHistory: Only Celia had possession before the investigation
evidence.discoveryConditions: Notice Celia's handbag or ask about her nervousness.

scenes.openingNarration: Rowan Vale stands beside a locked glass case, pale with indignation. His niece Celia sits near the fireplace with her handbag clutched tight. Thomas Greer, the valet, waits by the tea trolley with perfect posture. The study door has remained closed, and the rare watch has vanished.
likelyPlayerQuestions: When was the watch last seen?

safeAnswers.Who was in the room?: Rowan Vale, Celia Vale, and Thomas Greer were present.
safeAnswers.Was the door locked?: The study door was closed, and no one left after the watch was shown.
safeAnswers.Was the case locked?: The front lock appears intact. A careful inspection may reveal more.

hiddenFactsNotYetRevealed: Thomas loosened the rear hinge earlier.
The watch is inside the tallcase clock.

scenes.openingNarration: The display case is small, elegant, and apparently secure. The lock looks undamaged, but the back of the case deserves attention.
likelyPlayerQuestions: Can the case be opened another way?

safeAnswers.Is the lock damaged?: No. The front lock is intact.
safeAnswers.Who polished the case?: Rowan says Thomas polished the study earlier that afternoon.

hiddenFactsNotYetRevealed: Thomas used the rear hinge to remove the watch.

scenes.entryConditions: Player inspects or asks about the tallcase clock.
scenes.openingNarration: The tallcase clock stands sternly near the wall, its hands fixed at 8:13. A small oil cloth rests nearby.
likelyPlayerQuestions: Can I open the lower compartment?

safeAnswers.Who winds the clock?: Rowan says Thomas winds it every evening.
safeAnswers.Can I open the lower compartment?: Yes. With Rowan's permission, the small lower door can be opened.

hiddenFactsNotYetRevealed: The watch is hidden in the clock until the player listens or opens the compartment.

scenes.entryConditions: Player identifies Thomas and gives sufficient reasoning or discovers the watch in the clock.
scenes.openingNarration: The evidence no longer points to the front lock. It points to the hinge, the black thread, the clock oil, and the stopped clock.
likelyPlayerQuestions: How did he get the watch out?

safeAnswers.Was Celia involved?: No. Her pawnshop envelope was embarrassing but unrelated.

discoveryRules.triggerDescription: Player inspects the display case, especially the hinge or rear edge.
caseBoardUpdates: The front lock may be irrelevant; the rear hinge was tampered with.

discoveryRules.triggerDescription: Player inspects, listens to, or opens the tallcase clock.
caseBoardUpdates: The watch was hidden inside the tallcase clock.

discoveryRules.triggerDescription: Player asks Celia about the pawnshop envelope or her finances.
caseBoardUpdates: Celia has a money secret, but the envelope concerns her bracelet.

discoveryRules.triggerDescription: Player questions Thomas about his movements, clock duties, or the display case.
caseBoardUpdates: Thomas appears too aware of the stopped clock.

discoveryRules.triggerDescription: Player accuses Thomas with hinge, thread, oil, stopped clock, and hidden watch evidence.
caseBoardUpdates: Case solved: Thomas Greer stole the watch and hid it in the clock.

knownFacts: The Breguet pocket watch was last seen in the locked glass case at 7:55 p.m.
The study door remained closed after the watch was shown.
Only Rowan Vale, Celia Vale, and Thomas Greer were present.
The front lock on the display case appears intact.
openQuestions: How did the thief remove the watch without damaging the front lock?
Why did the clock stop at 8:13?

assetManifest.title: Study overview
assetManifest.discoveryConditions: Player requests an overview of the study.
assetManifest.textFallback: The study contains a writing desk, fireplace, display case, tallcase clock, tea trolley, green-shaded lamp, and rain-streaked window.

assetManifest.title: Locked display case
assetManifest.discoveryConditions: Player inspects or requests the display case.
assetManifest.textFallback: The case has an intact front lock, but the rear hinge is loose and freshly disturbed.

assetManifest.title: Tallcase clock
assetManifest.discoveryConditions: Player inspects or requests the clock.
assetManifest.textFallback: The tallcase clock has stopped at 8:13 and its lower latch shows a fresh scrape.

assetManifest.title: Breguet pocket watch
assetManifest.discoveryConditions: Player discovers the watch inside the clock.
assetManifest.textFallback: The missing gold pocket watch is wrapped in a polishing cloth inside the clock compartment.

knownRiskAreas: Ensure Thomas's opportunity in the one-room setting is clear but not overexplained too early.
Ensure Celia's red herring is clearable and does not unfairly outweigh physical evidence.
Ensure the Game Master does not reveal the watch in the clock before reasonable inspection or listening.
